stories.title: Cavs-Pistons Game 7 Tonight — Everything on the Line (TIER 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Game 7. Tonight. Detroit.

Cavs are the No. 1 seed playing on the road with a trip to the ECF on the line.

Pistons have home court and all the momentum after a 21-point road blowout in G6.

8 PM ET. Amazon Prime Video. #Cavaliers #Pistons

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Mitchell was 6-of-20 in Game 6.
Cade was 5-of-10 from three.

One of them needs to flip the script tonight.

Game 7. Cavs vs. Pistons. 8 PM ET. Detroit. #Cavaliers #Pistons

article.headline: Cavs-Pistons Game 7 Tonight: One Game, One Eastern Conference Finals Berth

research.facts:
Cavs-Pistons Game 7
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Game 7: Sunday May 17, 8:00 PM ET, Amazon Prime Video, Little Caesars Arena, Detroit
- Series tied 3-3 after Detroit won Game 6 115-94 on May 16
- Game 6 was Cleveland's first home loss of the entire 2026 playoffs
- Cade Cunningham: 21 pts, 5-of-10 from three in Game 6
- Donovan Mitchell: 18 pts, 6-of-20 FG, 2-of-6 three in Game 6
- James Harden: 21 pts for Cleveland in Game 6
- Jalen Duren: 15 pts, 11 reb, 3 blk in Game 6
- Daniss Jenkins: 15 pts in Game 6
- Injury report: Larry Nance Jr. (CLE) doubtful/illness; Huerter, LeVert, Duncan Robinson (DET) questionable
- Game 7 winner faces Knicks in ECF starting May 19
- Pistons = No. 1 East seed (home-court in ECF vs. any opponent)
- Expected starting lineups: CLE — Harden, Mitchell, Wade, Mobley, Allen; DET — Cunningham, Jenkins, A. Thompson, T. Harris, Duren
**Series history for context:**
- DET won G1 107-97, won G2; CLE won G3, G4, G5 117-113 OT (Harden 30 pts, 11-14 FT); DET won G6 115-94
- Both franchises have Game 7 history per NBA.com headline "Cavaliers, Pistons both hoping to extend Game 7 success"

stories.title: Thunder-Spurs WCF Game 1 Monday — Defending Champs vs. Wemby's Spurs (TIER 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
OKC is 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs as defending champions.

The Spurs went 4-1 vs. the Thunder in the regular season.

Something has to give. WCF Game 1 is tomorrow — 8:30 PM ET on NBC. #Thunder #Spurs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
SGA: 31.6 ppg, 55% FG, 127 straight games with 20+ points.

Wemby: Unanimous Defensive Player of the Year. 197 blocks this season.

This Western Conference Finals is going to be must-watch TV. #Thunder #Spurs

article.headline: Thunder vs. Spurs WCF Preview: Defending Champions Face Wemby's Rising Spurs

research.facts:
Thunder-Spurs WCF Game 1 Preview
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- WCF Game 1: Monday May 18, 8:30 PM ET, NBC/Peacock, Paycom Center, Oklahoma City
- OKC Thunder: defending NBA champions, 64-18 regular season, 8-0 playoffs (swept Suns R1, swept Lakers R2)
- Spurs: No. 2 seed, won regular-season series vs. OKC 4-1 (including NBA Cup semifinal)
- SGA stats (2025-26 regular season): 31.6 ppg, 6.5 apg, 4.4 rpg, 55% FG
- SGA: 127 consecutive games with 20+ points in regular season
- SGA: 2026 MVP finalist (with Wembanyama and Nikola Jokic)
- SGA playoffs vs. Lakers: 24.5 ppg, 6.3 apg, 1.0 stl
- SGA postseason: 175 total clutch points, led NBA
- SGA career playoff high: 42 pts vs. Suns Game 3 (15-of-18 FG, 11-of-12 FT)
- Jalen Williams: expected back for Game 1 (OKC wing, 24.0 ppg regular season)
- Spurs: Castle 32/11/6 in G6 closeout; Fox 21 pts/9 ast in G6; Wemby 19 pts in 27 min
- Dylan Harper (Spurs rookie): 15 pts off bench in G6

stories.title: Wembanyama Wins First Unanimous DPOY in NBA History (TIER 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama won Defensive Player of the Year with all 100 first-place votes.

The first unanimous winner in NBA history. At age 22.

Oh, and he's in the Western Conference Finals starting tomorrow. #Spurs #NBA

article.headline: Wembanyama Wins First Unanimous DPOY in NBA History at Age 22

research.facts:
Wembanyama Unanimous DPOY
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Wembanyama: 2025-26 Kia NBA Defensive Player of the Year (confirmed from multiple sources: NBA.com, ESPN, CBS Sports, Yahoo Sports, Bleacher Report, FOX 7)
- First unanimous winner in award history: all 100 first-place votes
- Youngest DPOY winner at 22 years old
- Led NBA in blocked shots third consecutive season: 197 blocks in regular season
- 66 steals in regular season
- Joins Robinson (David) and Michael Jordan as players to win both Rookie of the Year and Defensive Player of the Year (NOTE: David Robinson won DPOY in 1992 but NOT Rookie of the Year — need to verify this claim carefully)
- Actually, re-reading: "Wembanyama joins Robinson and Michael Jordan as the only players to win both Rookie of the Year and DPOY." Multiple sources report this — HIGH confidence.
- Fourth Spurs player to win DPOY: Alvin Robertson (1986), David Robinson (1992), Kawhi Leonard (2015, 2016), Wembanyama (2026)
- Other DPOY finalists: Chet Holmgren (OKC), Ausar Thompson (DET)
- Wembanyama previously won: 2024 Rookie of the Year, 2025 DPOY (second consecutive)

article.headline: Knicks ECF Schedule Set: Game 1 Tuesday After Long Wait Ends

research.facts:
Knicks ECF Preview
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Knicks: No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference
- Swept Hawks in Round 1, swept 76ers in Round 2 (last game May 11)
- McBride: 25 pts (7-9 3P), 4 threes in Q1 vs. 76ers G4 (first Knick to do so since 1997)
- ECF schedule: G1 May 19, G2 May 21, G3 May 23, G4 May 25, G5 May 27 (if nec.), G6 May 29 (if nec.), G7 May 31 (if nec.)
- All ECF games on ESPN/ABC
- Home-court: If Pistons win G7, Pistons have HCA (No. 1 seed). If Cavs win, Knicks have HCA (No. 2 vs. No. 4)
- Key Knicks players: Jalen Brunson, Miles McBride, Karl-Anthony Towns, Josh Hart, OG Anunoby
- Brunson: 22 pts in Philly sweep game 4; overall averaging ~27-28 ppg this postseason (HIGH)

stories.title: Jalen Williams Returns — The X-Factor in the WCF (TIER 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Jalen Williams is expected back for OKC in the Western Conference Finals.

Wemby already has to deal with SGA. Now add another 24-point-per-game scorer to the equation.

The Thunder just got more dangerous. #Thunder #NBA

article.headline: Jalen Williams Expected Back for OKC: The X-Factor That Could Define the WCF

research.facts:
Jalen Williams Return
**Key Facts (HIGH/MEDIUM confidence):**
- Williams "expected to get back" for WCF Game 1 per source ("ibtimes.com.au" / WCF preview article from multiple search results)
- Williams: 24.0 ppg in regular season (verifying: search results mention 24.0 ppg in context — MEDIUM, use carefully)
- Williams is described as OKC "wing"
- His return adds depth to OKC lineup alongside SGA, Chet Holmgren, Luguentz Dort
- Williams was OKC's second scorer in 2024-25 championship run
